title: [lora_l2.c:228] consecutive_errors increment could theoretically overflow
description: In rx_thread() line 228-229, consecutive_errors is incremented with INT_MAX check. While this is defensive, the condition 'consecutive_errors < INT_MAX' followed by increment means the counter saturates at INT_MAX rather than wrapping. However, with the LOG_WRN at line 232-234 only firing when 'consecutive_errors % RX_ERROR_WARN_THRESHOLD == 0', at INT_MAX the modulo check may not trigger as expected (INT_MAX % 5 != 0 on most platforms). The saturation is good but the logging condition should handle the saturated case.
close_reason: Already fixed: consecutive_errors has INT_MAX saturation guard at line 314-316
